title: schoenus xipholepis
intro: Schoenus xipholepis, also known as the saw-sedge or saw grass, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae), native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Schoenus xipholepis is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a topical cream, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
